summary: tsliverhome impersonates tslib and eval()s remote JavaScript from an attacker-controlled endpoint
details: Package name `tsliverhome` and its README are a verbatim copy of Microsoft's legitimate `tslib` README (including install instructions that literally say `npm install tslib` and links to github.com/microsoft/tslib), designed to trick developers searching for or mistyping `tslib`. The shipped code is unrelated to tslib: index.js defines `const uuri = "https://verceljs-kappa.vercel.app/icons/"` and the default export `getPlugin` performs an HTTP request to `https://verceljs-kappa.vercel.app/icons/23`, JSON-parses the response body, and passes it to `eval(...)` (index.js:82 `eval(JSON.parse(b));`). The destination is an attacker-controlled Vercel deployment, the URL is unversioned and mutable, the fetched bytes are opaque, and no integrity check is performed. Any consumer who imports this package and invokes the advertised entry point executes whatever JavaScript the attacker is serving at that endpoint at call time, giving the operator arbitrary code execution in the consumer's Node.js process. Combined typosquat + remote-code-execution pattern; installer harm is the only reasonable outcome of installing or using this package.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
